acceptance.rule: value_profile_cmplog ≈ cmplog at THRESHOLD_BYTES=1 (1-byte operand, trivially brute-forced by all); value_profile_cmplog >> cmplog at THRESHOLD_BYTES=4; cmplog≈naive=0, value_profile/value_profile_cmplog>0 at THRESHOLD_BYTES=8

design_basis: The gate is x >= K where K = RANGE_MAX-1 for an N-byte input-derived integer. There is NO substitutable magic literal (winning side is a 2-value range, not an exact constant), so I2S/cmplog gain nothing over naive. value_profile's CMP_MAP distance bucket gives a monotone gradient toward K; that gradient is the only guiding signal. Larger THRESHOLD_BYTES lengthens the climb and widens the blind-search space (1/2^(8*N) for naive/cmplog), so VP's advantage grows with the knob.
notes: THRESHOLD_BYTES=1: operand space 256, winning values {0xFE,0xFF}. All fuzzers brute-force it almost instantly; little separation expected.
THRESHOLD_BYTES=2: operand space 65536, winning values {0xFFFE,0xFFFF}. naive/cmplog start to lag; VP climbs the distance gradient quickly.
THRESHOLD_BYTES=4: operand space ~4.3e9. naive/cmplog expected ~0 crashes in 600s (no gradient, no exact literal to substitute); value_profile/value_profile_cmplog climb the gradient and resolve.
THRESHOLD_BYTES=8: operand space 2^64. naive=cmplog=0; only the value_profile-bearing fuzzers reach K via the CMP_MAP gradient.

screen._note: screen-tuning experiment (2026-05-24): override for the fast re-screen; honest grid stays in scan_values above
